verifier_issues.message: attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] could not be parsed (No module named 'openpyxl'). Grader relied on expert_memo.md for semantic claims. Recommend opening the workbook in a spreadsheet runtime to validate hidden cells, external links, and in-sheet formulas.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Grader evidence is grounded in the provided expert_memo.md (which is present and whose numeric claims are internally consistent with the memo). Scores are internally consistent across deterministic precheck and model grading. No hidden-reference leakage detected. One limitation: the SLO_burn_1.xlsx workbook could not be parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so workbook-level checks (hidden cells, external links, in-sheet formula verification) could not be validated; runtime spreadsheet verification is recommended.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.recommendation: Accept the grader result as supported by the memo and artifact manifest, but perform a spreadsheet runtime verification (open the xlsx in a spreadsheet tool) to confirm hidden-cell/formula/external-link checks before final publishing.

raw_model_output.verifier_issues_summary: Grader evidence is grounded in the provided expert_memo.md (which is present and whose numeric claims are internally consistent with the memo). Scores are internally consistent across deterministic precheck and model grading. No hidden-reference leakage detected. One limitation: the SLO_burn_1.xlsx workbook could not be parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so workbook-level checks (hidden cells, external links, in-sheet formula verification) could not be validated; runtime spreadsheet verification is recommended.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: 1) Inconsistent internal fields: multiple contradictory score/passed-criteria fields exist (e.g., top-level final_score 0.775 vs deterministic precheck score 1.0 and differing passed_criteria lists). 2) Partial grounding: expert_memo text preview is available and supports most semantic claims, but the .xlsx was not parseable (openpyxl_unavailable) so numeric recomputations and workbook formulas were not programmatically verified. 3) Minor unsupported-claim risk: grader asserts combined-remediation feasibility concerns correctly (e.g., two O1 applications exceed max_budget) but also awards per-criterion full credit in some places inconsistent with later rubric_item_scores; normalization of these claims is needed.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

recommendations: Enable .xlsx parsing (install/openpyxl) and re-run the automated verifier to confirm numeric formulas and recomputations in SLO_burn_1.xlsx.
Normalize grader output: consolidate and de-duplicate score, passed_criteria, and rubric-item results so a single authoritative set of fields remains.
Have the grader/memo add explicit combined-cost feasibility calculations (sum of recommended remediation costs) with workbook cell references to remove ambiguity about 'within_budget' labels.
unsupported_claims: Numeric recomputations and formula-level verification in SLO_burn_1.xlsx were not programmatically validated due to openpyxl_unavailable; any grader claims that rely on recomputed workbook numbers are therefore only textually supported by expert_memo.md, not verified against the binary workbook.
Internal grading fields are inconsistent (multiple sections report different passed_criteria and score values), so some claimed passes/fails are not fully substantiated by a single consistent record.
